name: Hot & Sour Soup 4
recipeIngredient: 1 quarter-sized slice of fresh, peeled ginger, bruised
2 tablespoons water chestnut flour mixed with 4 tablespoons water
1 egg, lightly beaten with 1 teaspoon oil
recipeInstructions:
1. Skinned and cut into matchstick strips or dry sherry. S.C.
2. Use cornstarch if unavailable. S.C.
3. Cut into strips 1/4 inch thick and 1 1/2 inches long
4. Cover black mushrooms, lily buds, and wood ears with water until soft and pliable, about 30 minutes. Squeeze dry.
5. Remove and discard stems from black mushrooms; julienne.
6. Remove and discard hard ends of lily buds.
7. Remove and discard any hard clusters in centers of wood ears. Roll each wood ear into a tight curl, cut cross-wise into thin strips.
8. Mix marinade ingredients. Toss with the chicken strips and set aside.
9. Heat wok over medium-high heat. When hot, add oil and tilt wok to coat sides.
10. Add salt, ginger, and chicken. Stir-fry for 1 minute or until chicken turns white. Remove chicken and set aside.
11. Increase to high heat. Add black mushrooms, lily buds, and wood ears to hot oil. Stir-fry until fragrant, about 1 minute.
12. Add soy sauce and Shao Hsing wine; stir-fry for 5 seconds.
13. Add stock and bring to a boil. Reduce heat to low and simmer for 10 minutes.
14. Bring back to a boil. Add water chestnut flour mixture. Stir continuously until thickened (about 30 seconds).
15. Remove from heat. Slowly pour egg into soup in a circular motion while stirring slowly with chopsticks to form silky ribbons.
16. Add tofu and chicken.
17. Pour half of the Hot and Sour Seasoning into a shallow soup tureen. Ladle soup over sauce, scatter green onions on top, and float remaining Hot and Sour Seasoning on top of soup. Stir gently.
Serves 4 as a meal in itself.
